mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production index signals expanding manufacturing output and revenue growth, which directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial Production month-over-month increases fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, further anticipated increases tighten financial conditions, compress equity multiples, and generate negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns as liquidity is withdrawn.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered monthly instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signals a robust consumer demand-employment feedback loop, accelerating earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of concurrent instances.

mechanism_narrative: During inflationary regimes, a rising Consumer Price Index triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, increases equity risk premiums, and generates negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is rising month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative on average across the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in new private housing starts indicates weakening construction activity and downstream demand for materials and labor, forecasting negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Backtest shows negative 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that drives wage growth and consumer spending optimism, overwhelming margin concerns and generating positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive (mean annualized return <= 0 over the triggered sample).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising retail sales and a flat or falling federal funds rate indicates demand growth without restrictive monetary offset, producing positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns as liquidity supports consumption.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is flat or down
failure_condition: The joint condition triggers fewer than 30 observations over the sample, or positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing 4-quarter average signals accelerating economic momentum, lifting broad-based earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter trailing average, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p-value > 0.10).
